title: NOC 12100 – Executive Assistants | GTR Immigration
description: Executive Assistants play a pivotal role in senior administration across government bodies, corporations, and major organizations in Canada. Classified under NOC 12100, this occupation enjoys strong national demand and offers realistic pathways to Permanent Residence (PR) through Canada’s skilled worker immigration programs.

classification.teer: 2 – Occupations usually requiring a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more years; or supervisory occupations

duties: Establish and coordinate administrative policies for senior officials and boards
Review memoranda and reports and prepare executive summaries
Organize agendas and logistics for board and committee meetings
Conduct research and compile briefing materials
Represent executives in meetings with stakeholders
Liaise with internal departments and external organizations
employment_requirements: A bachelor’s degree in public administration, political science, or a related field is usually required
Prior experience in a senior administrative role
job_outlook: From 2022 to 2031, Canada is expected to generate 19,700 new Executive Assistant positions, while approximately 18,100 job seekers will enter the field—creating balanced yet favourable prospects for internationally trained professionals.
additional_information: Executive Assistants are increasingly sought after across both public and private sectors as organizations expand and executive operations become more complex.

Strong post-pandemic hiring momentumStable long-term administrative careersEligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs)Employer-driven immigration opportunitiesAnnual earnings typically ranging from $38,700 to $85,200
Universal public healthcareCompetitive compensation packagesProfessional work environmentsStrong labour protectionsWork-life balanceMulticultural communitiesClear pathways from work permits to PR
